definition: A series of thoughts, images, and sensations occurring in a person's mind during sleep
example: I have a dream that one day this nation will rise up and live out the true meaning of its creed: 'We hold these truths to be self-evident, that all men are created equal.'
speech: I Have a Dream (March on Washington)

definition: A system of religious belief; a faith
example: I have a dream that one day this nation will rise up and live out the true meaning of its creed: 'We hold these truths to be self-evident, that all men are created equal.'
speech: I Have a Dream (March on Washington)

definition: Lack of fairness or justice
example: Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere. We are caught in an inescapable network of mutuality, tied in a single garment of destiny.
speech: Letter from Birmingham Jail (written during imprisonment)

definition: The quality of being mutual; sharing of a feeling, action, or relationship
example: Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere. We are caught in an inescapable network of mutuality, tied in a single garment of destiny.
speech: Letter from Birmingham Jail (written during imprisonment)

definition: The events that will necessarily happen to a particular person or thing in the future
example: Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere. We are caught in an inescapable network of mutuality, tied in a single garment of destiny.
speech: Letter from Birmingham Jail (written during imprisonment)

definition: Importance requiring swift action
example: We have also come to this hallowed spot to remind America of the fierce urgency of Now.
speech: I Have a Dream (March on Washington)

definition: A policy of gradual reform rather than sudden change or revolution
example: This is no time to engage in the luxury of cooling off or to take the tranquilizing drug of gradualism.
speech: I Have a Dream

definition: The use of peaceful means, not force, to bring about political or social change
example: Nonviolence is the answer to the crucial political and moral questions of our time.
speech: I Have a Dream (March on Washington)

definition: Prolonged cruel or unjust treatment or exercise of authority
example: I have a dream that one day even the state of Mississippi, a state sweltering with the heat of injustice, sweltering with the heat of oppression, will be transformed into an oasis of freedom and justice.
speech: I Have a Dream (March on Washington)

definition: A fertile spot in a desert, where water is found
example: I have a dream that one day even the state of Mississippi, a state sweltering with the heat of injustice, sweltering with the heat of oppression, will be transformed into an oasis of freedom and justice.
speech: I Have a Dream (March on Washington)

definition: The mental and moral qualities distinctive to an individual
example: I have a dream that my four little children will one day live in a nation where they will not be judged by the color of their skin but by the content of their character.
speech: I Have a Dream (March on Washington)

definition: Intense dislike; hatred
example: Darkness cannot drive out darkness; only light can do that. Hate cannot drive out hate; only love can do that.
speech: I Have a Dream (March on Washington)

definition: A small, narrow river
example: We will not be satisfied until justice rolls down like waters and righteousness like a mighty stream.
speech: I Have a Dream (March on Washington)

definition: The quality of being morally right or justifiable
example: We will not be satisfied until justice rolls down like waters and righteousness like a mighty stream.
speech: I Have a Dream

definition: Not heard or listened to
example: A riot is the language of the unheard.
source: Speech at Grosse Pointe High School
speech: The Other America

definition: Extreme fear
example: We are now faced with the fact that tomorrow is today. We are confronted with the fierce urgency of now.
speech: I Have a Dream (March on Washington)

definition: The absence of sound or noise
example: In the end, we will remember not the words of our enemies, but the silence of our friends.
speech: I Have a Dream (March on Washington)

definition: The top of a mountain
example: I have been to the mountaintop. And I don't mind. Like anybody, I would like to live a long life.
speech: I Have a Dream (March on Washington)

definition: High renown or honor won by notable achievements
example: Mine eyes have seen the glory of the coming of the Lord.
speech: I Have a Dream (March on Washington)

definition: A feeling of expectation and desire for a particular thing to happen
example: We must accept finite disappointment, but never lose infinite hope.
speech: Various sermons compiled in Strength to Love
